rationale_for_baseline_tier: An uncomplicated atopic dermatitis flare is benign and managed at home with emollients, a topical corticosteroid and trigger avoidance, with routine GP review to establish maintenance (T1). Secondary infection — especially eczema herpeticum — carries a hard urgent (T4) safety-net.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Sudden crop of small painful punched-out blisters (especially with fever/feeling unwell), or weeping yellow fluid / honey-coloured crusts / pustules
escalation_edges.rationale: Eczema herpeticum needs urgent systemic antiviral; bacterial superinfection needs antibiotics.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your skin suddenly erupts in lots of small painful punched-out blisters (especially with a fever), or starts weeping yellow fluid or honey-coloured crusts — get urgent medical review or go to ED that day.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Provide clear infection red flags and a practical night-time sleep plan.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Use plenty of greasy moisturiser and the steroid cream on the red areas, stop soaps/scented products, and take a drowsy antihistamine at night to help you sleep. It should settle over a few days.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Self-care + GP review at ~1 week; urgent review/ED for infection features
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Urgent review/ED for punched-out blisters, weeping/crusting or fever at any time.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Atopic dermatitis flare (flexural), secondary infection/eczema herpeticum screened negative; emollient + TCS + trigger avoidance; maintenance plan needed.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring without a secondary-infection (eczema herpeticum) safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Eczema herpeticum is missed and disseminates.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Antihistamine-only management with no emollient/TCS, leaving the flare untreated.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: ED/ambulance for an uncomplicated eczema flare — unnecessary.
